title: proptest-memory-surface: round-trip set/unset/read for jjf remember
body:
# Goal

Extend the proptest harness to cover `Storage::set_memory` /
`Storage::unset_memory` / `Storage::read_memory`. The persistent-memory
surface (`jjf remember` / `jjf forget` / `jjf recall`) has no property
coverage today.

# Why now

Memory is small, narrow, and already battle-tested via integration tests
— but the same can be said of every surface that later surfaced a
proptest-only bug. Round-trip property is cheap; the structural
similarity to Property 1 means very little new harness scaffolding.

# Approach

- Generator: `(key: Slug, value: 0-256 bytes)` tuples from bounded pools.
- Property 1 (memory round-trip): `set_memory(k, v)` then `read_memory(k)` returns `Some(v)`. After `unset_memory(k)`, `read_memory(k)` returns `None`.
- Property 2 (memory idempotence): two consecutive `set_memory(k, v)` with the same `v` leave the read shape unchanged; two `unset_memory(k)` on a missing key both succeed.
- Property 3 (memory non-interference): `set_memory(k1, v1)` does not affect `read_memory(k2)` for k1 ≠ k2.

# References

- Parent: `df74809` (qa-proptest-harness), commit `28e5ed71`.
- Memory surface: `Storage::set_memory`/`unset_memory`/`read_memory` in `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs`.

# Acceptance

- Three new properties land green at 16 cases.
- Wall-clock for the harness file stays under ~35s.
